title: Expression and purification of Tribolium castaneum orthologue of PINK1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d067q","text":"Mutations in PINK1 (protein kinase) and Parkin (ubiquitin E3 ligase) have been linked to familial early-onset Parkinson’s disease. PINK1 phosphorylates ubiquitin and the N-terminal ubiquitin-like domain (Ubl) of Parkin at a conserved Serine65 residue in both proteins to initiate mitophagy. Direct measurement of human PINK1 kinase activity has been challenging due to poor expression and low activity in vitro. The discovery of catalytically active insect PINK1 orthologues including Tribolium castaneum PINK1 (TcPINK1) and Pediculus humanus corporis PINK1 (PhcPINK1) has enabled elaboration of robust assays for measurement of PINK1 kinase activity (Woodroof et al., 2011). These assays can be employed for the study of human pathogenic PINK1 mutations and in vitro structural and functional studies of PINK1 (Kumar et al., 2017). Herein, we describe in detail a protocol for expression and purification of TcPINK1 for crystallisation as well as for in vitro characterisation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Mix 1.0 µL of pET 6His sumo codon optimised TcPINK1 150-end ΔI261-L270 S205E E527A K528A plasmid ( 50 ng/µl ) with 50 µL of the competent BL21(DE3) cells.
Incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .
Heat shock the cells in a water bath at 42 °C for 00:00:50 .
Transfer the vial back onto ice and add 200 µL SOC and mix gently.
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 37 °C for recovery.
Plate all of the cells onto a LB-agar plate supplemented with 10 µg/ml ampicillin.
Leave the plate overnight in a 37 °C incubator.
Pick a single colony using a sterile pipette tip from the plate and drop the tip into 100 mL LB medium, supplemented with 10 µg/ml ampicillin.
Incubate at 37 °C with 180 rpm, °C, - 200 rpm, °C, rotational shaking Overnight . The culture medium should become totally dense or turbid the following morning.
Decant 6 x 1L TB medium into 6 x 2L autoclaved conical flasks.
To each flask add 50 µg of ampicillin per litre. Prepare the stock antibiotics as 1000- fold to ensure consistent supplementation.
After 04:00:00 check the OD 600 by taking 1 mL of randomly selected flasks and a fresh TB medium as blank using a WPA cell densitometer or a spectrometer.
When the temperature drops to 16 °C , induce protein expression by adding 100 µM of IPTG (From 1 M stock) and allow the cells to continue to grow for a further 16 - 20 h (usually Overnight ).
The following morning, decant the content of the 2 L conical flasks into 1 L Beckman centrifuge pots.
At the end of the run, carefully decant the supernatant back into the flasks. The flasks can then be autoclaved and cleaned.
The cell pellets (usually between 3 mL – 5 mL volume) are then resuspended using the lysis buffer.

To do this, 10 mL of cold lysis buffer is added to the pellet in each of the pot supplemented with 30 µL of a 10 mg/mL Leupeptin solution, 60 µL of a 0.5 M Pefabloc solution and 100 µL of 1 mg/mL DNase

Transfer the 1 L pots to the Infors incubator and the temperature still at 16 °C and the rotation lowered to 120 rpm, °C, . Leave the pots to rotate for 00:30:00 , after which the cell pellet should have completely resuspended. If there are still some pellet left, pipette up and down close to the bottom of the pots.

Transfer the resuspended cells into 50 mL falcon tubes with 30 mL aliquots and flash freeze in liquid nitrogen (this helps to also break up the cells) and stored in -20 °C for short time storage or in -80 °C for longer storage.

Best to prepare all the buffers a day before and keep in the cold room When you are ready for the purification, slowly thaw the cells suspension on On ice or in cold water. Make sure the cells are On ice always or best work in the cold room

Once the cells are resuspended move to lyse the cells using a sonicator or cell disruptor. Sonicate On ice , using a probe sonicator (Settings: 6 – 8 pulses of 00:00:10 with 00:00:15 pauses. Set the amplitude to 45%. Please note the lysate will warm up slightly, but make sure it does not warm up over 18 °C

Transfer the sonicated suspension into 40 mL Beckman centrifuge tubes and collect the cell debris by centrifugation at 4 °C for 00:40:00 at 20.000 x g, °C, using a 25.50 rotor in a Beckman Avanti centrifuge.
Load 5.0 mL Ni-agarose slurry on a 60 mL gravity column and wash three times with Milli Q water and once with the binding buffer. 5 ml of a 50% slurry, sufficient for a 6 L expression
Add the Ni-agarose slurry to the cell lysate in an appropriate size Duran bottle and incubate the mix on a roller shaker for 02:00:00 in the cold room. Do not filter the lysate
After the incubation period, collect the beads by passing the mixture through a 60 mL gravity flow chromatography column, wash the beads thoroughly using the washing buffer.
Once clean elute the protein using the elution buffer. Check that all the protein has been eluted by checking with Coomassie

Cleave PINK1 from His-sumo by dialysing the eluted protein against the dialysis buffer using the 3.5 kDa dialysis bag in the presence of His-Snep1 protease Overnight in the cold room. 1 mg of His-SENP1 per 25 mg of PINK1 The following day perform negative pull down to get rid of snep1 and sumo.
To remove His-sumo and His-senp1 from the cleaved protein, perform negative pull down.

To do this, still in the cold room, pack a 5 mL PolyPrep chromatography column with 2 mL nickel beads, equilibrate with binding buffer and gently by pipetting pass the dialysed protein mixture through the packed Ni-beads. This traps the Histidine tagged proteins and your cleaved PINK1 protein passes through
Collect the flow through into a new falcon tube.

This is the tricky bit as the protein tends to aggregate/oligomerises and this make concentrating the protein using the vivaspin concentrator a bit difficult (increased agitation of the protein due to long concentration time). To concentrate the protein, consider using PEG20,000. To do this, the protein is dialysed against the PEG dialysis buffer. This takes between 05:00:00 to Overnight depending on the starting volume Immerse the dialysis bag with protein in some peg2000 powder will equally do the magic in reducing the volume The idea is that you want to reduce the volume drastically before moving to vivaspin concentrator to reduce the level of agitation on the protein.

To obtain a crystallization grade protein, size exclusion chromatography is always the last purification step. This can be done on any of the Akta systems at 4 °C on a superdex75 pg (26/600) or superdex200 pg 16/600 column Equilibrate the column with 2-column volume of the column size with the SEC buffer. Before loading the sample clean the Akta sample loop with 2CV of the loop size first with water followed by the SEC buffer.
Inject the sample and run the program (usually 1 ml/min flowrate and 3 mL fractions). Using this program on superdex 75 column gives a chromatogram shown in Figure 1a. Fig1A.PNG
Run 5 µL of fractions within the peak (Figure 1a) on a 12% Tris Glycine SDS-polyacrylamide gel at the end of the run and stain with small amount Instant Blue.
After detaining a single band corresponding to ~ 48 kDa should be visible as shown in Figure 1b. Fig1B.PNG

Pull together the cleanest fractions within the peak and concentrate using the vivaspin 30 kDa cut off to at least 10 mg/mL or less this might take between 02:00:00 - 03:00:00 depending on your initial volume. Here it is expected that you have less volume, however if your volume is large you might consider using the PEG method to reduce the volume before finishing off with vivaspin concentrator.
Make aliquots and flash freeze in liquid nitrogen and stored in -80 °C .
Incubate at 37 °C , using an Infors Shaker-Incubator set at 180 rpm, °C, .
Then add 10 mL of the Overnight culture into each flask.
When the OD 600 has reached 0.6 - 0.8, lower the temperature of the incubator to 16 °C and continue the incubation for at least another 01:00:00 .
Close the pots tightly and sediment the cells at 4 °C by centrifugation for 00:30:00 at 4000 rpm, °C, using a Beckman J6 centrifuge with the 6 x 1 L rotor.
Check that the beads are clean by adding 20 µL of the flow through to 100 µL of Coomassie.
creator: Miratul Muqit
